what differs a language model AI from a AI

A language model AI is a type of artificial intelligence (AI) that is specifically designed to process and generate human language. It is trained on a large dataset of text and is able to generate responses to prompts or questions based on that training.

AI, on the other hand, is a broader term that encompasses a wide range of technologies and systems that are capable of performing tasks that would normally require human intelligence, such as understanding speech, recognizing images, or making decisions.

Language models are a type of AI that is focused specifically on processing and generating human language, whereas other types of AI may be focused on tasks such as image recognition, natural language processing, or decision-making.

In short, a language model AI is a type of AI that is designed specifically for natural language processing tasks, while AI is a broader term that encompasses a wide range of technologies and systems that are capable of performing tasks that would normally require human intelligence.
